reference_categories.technical_architecture: Infrastructure components (API gateway, cache, database, message queue)
reference_categories.endpoints: API endpoint definitions for fleet, mission control, robot commands, and telemetry
reference_categories.libraries: Code libraries for HTTP client, state management, UI components, validation, WebRTC
reference_categories.protocols: Communication protocols for authentication, robot communication, and telemetry formats
reference_categories.data_models: Detailed model schemas for maintenance, missions, parts, robots, telemetry, users
reference_categories.platforms: Platform specifications for AWS infrastructure, mobile, and web platforms
reference_categories.business_logic: Detailed workflows and rules for operations like emergency stops, mission assignment, telemetry processing
reference_categories.acceptance_criteria: Success criteria for features with functional, performance, and integration requirements
reference_categories.state_mutations: State change specifications for critical operations
reference_categories.content: Brand messaging, button text, and interface labels
reference_categories.colors: Color system definitions with semantic names and hex values
reference_categories.typography: Font specifications, weights, sizes, and type scales
reference_categories.spacing: Layout and component spacing systems for consistent UI
reference_categories.ui_templates: Reusable UI patterns for buttons, cards, modals, and tables

entity_descriptions.project: Top-level container representing the entire software project or system
entity_descriptions.requirements: Functional or non-functional specifications that the system must satisfy
entity_descriptions.interfaces: User interface screens and pages in the application
entity_descriptions.features: Distinct functionality or capability provided to users
entity_descriptions.actions: Specific user interactions or system operations
entity_descriptions.components: Reusable building blocks of the system architecture
entity_descriptions.presentation: Visual and layout aspects of user interface components
entity_descriptions.behavior: Logic and state management for component interactions
entity_descriptions.data_models: Structure and schema definitions for data entities
entity_descriptions.users: Actors or roles that interact with the system
entity_descriptions.objectives: High-level goals or outcomes that users want to achieve

reference_notes.purpose: References are terminal graph nodes used for template generation and detailed specifications
reference_notes.schema: Each reference category uses a flexible schema tailored to its specific needs
reference_notes.naming: Reference keys use plaintext naming (not key_object_notation) for final nodes
reference_notes.usage: Entities in the graph can reference these nodes for implementation details and specifications
